qa_pairs.question: How can the run-time memory parameters for the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) be explicitly controlled?
qa_pairs.answer: The run-time memory parameters for the JVM can be explicitly controlled on the command line.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two syntaxes for specifying Java's initial and maximum heap sizes on the command line?
Provide an example command to set Java's initial heap size to 256 megabytes and maximum heap size to 4 gigabytes.
Which flag can be used to display all the command-line options that the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) will run with?
qa_pairs.answer: The flag `-XX:+PrintCommandLineFlags` can be used to display all command-line options the JVM will run with.

qa_pairs.question: Give an example of a command that sets initial and maximum heap sizes and also prints all command-line flags.
